title: Uruparia sessilifructus
uses: Uruparia sessilifructus is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and its medicinal properties have been documented in traditional folk medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. However, there is limited scientific research on its efficacy and safety for human consumption. In traditional medicine, Uruparia sessilifructus is often used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Further studies are needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and benefits.

Uraria picta, also known as Pictorial Uraria, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Uraria picta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uraria picta include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
